title: SEP Provider Response
description: The response from a stream provider to a consumer intent request. Contains a payload of enrichment envelopes, confidence scoring, a suggested session arc, and the provider's capability declarations. Supports multiple response types including payloads, errors, and redirects.

sep_version.description: The version of the Stream Exchange Protocol this response conforms to.

provider_id.description: The unique identifier of the stream provider issuing this response.

response_type.description: The type of response. 'payload': a successful response with content items. 'error': an error condition. 'redirect': the consumer should re-issue the request to a different endpoint.

unit_interval.description: A normalized score between 0 and 1 inclusive.

enrichment_envelope_ref.description: A full SEP enrichment envelope for a content item.

payload.description: The content payload returned by the provider. Contains enrichment envelopes, count metadata, a confidence score for the overall match, and an optional suggested session arc.

items.description: An array of enrichment envelopes matching the consumer's intent, ordered by relevance.

total_available.description: The total number of items in the provider's index that matched the consumer's intent, before pagination.

returned.description: The number of items actually returned in this response.

confidence.description: The provider's overall confidence that this payload satisfies the consumer's intent.

suggested_arc.description: A suggested session arc — an ordered sequence of category labels that the provider recommends for optimal pacing and narrative structure.

state_token.description: An opaque token the consumer can include in subsequent requests to enable stateful multi-round exchanges without the provider storing user profiles.

error.description: Error details when the response_type is 'error'.

retry_after_seconds.description: If rate-limited, the number of seconds the consumer should wait before retrying.

redirect.description: Redirect details when the response_type is 'redirect'.

target_url.description: The URL the consumer should re-issue its request to.

reason.description: A human-readable explanation for the redirect.

capabilities.description: The provider's capability declarations. Informs the consumer what exchange models and features this provider supports.

supports_stateful.description: Whether the provider supports stateful multi-round exchanges via state tokens.

supports_full_index_browse.description: Whether the provider supports Model B (full meta-index browse), allowing the consumer to query the entire metadata index directly.

supports_telemetry_exchange.description: Whether the provider accepts telemetry data from consumers who opt in.

max_payload_size.description: The maximum number of enrichment envelopes the provider will return in a single response.
